mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL continues to rise, real policy rates fall and inflation compensation rises; this combination pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next month as markets price less accommodation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury-yield changes are negative in most instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% while CPIAUCSL is still rising, or if the sign is not positive at better than chance in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor-market deterioration and weaker growth expectations, which should pull the 10-year Treasury yield lower within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 10-year yield rises after repeated UNRATE increase triggers, or if triggered events fail to produce negative yield changes more often than not over walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger household income and retail demand, lifting growth expectations and term premium, which should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not align with higher 20-day Treasury-yield outcomes, or if the interaction adds no predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls sharply while HOUST also contracts, the economy is signaling weaker current and forward real activity; that mix should lower the 10-year Treasury yield over the next month as growth and inflation expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive declines in INDPRO and HOUST are followed by flat or rising Treasury yields in a majority of cases, or if the joint condition is not stronger than either decline alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a sustained easing cycle, a rising CPIAUCSL with unchanged FEDFUNDS lifts inflation expectations faster than policy rates, so the 10-year Treasury yield should rise within five trading days as bond investors reprice nominal returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL accelerations with flat FEDFUNDS do not precede short-horizon yield increases, or if the effect disappears when policy is already tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A further rise in UNRATE from already elevated levels indicates recession risk and reduced inflation pressure, which should continue to push the 10-year Treasury yield downward over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if high-and-rising unemployment is not followed by lower 60-day Treasury yields, or if the relationship flips positive during recessionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a consumer-led recovery, simultaneous increases in RSAFS and PAYEMS raise nominal activity expectations and improve risk appetite, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of joint growth in sales and payrolls do not correspond to higher Treasury yields, or if the sign is neutral after controlling for single-series trends.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing downturn, weak INDPRO combined with soft HOUST marks fading real-side momentum and lower capacity pressure, so the 10-year Treasury yield should fall within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in industrial production and housing starts is not followed by lower yield direction, or if the pattern only works in isolated episodes and not across walk-forward tests.
